title: AgentWatch signatures registry
description: Schema for signatures.json — the public registry of AI agent detection rules consumed by the AgentWatch macOS app.

schemaVersion.description: Bump when the schema shape changes in a backward-incompatible way. The app rejects unknown major versions.

registryVersion.description: Calendar-versioned identifier for the registry contents (e.g. 2026-04-25 or 2026-04-25.2 if multiple updates land same day). Used to decide whether a fetched copy beats the cached one.

providers.description: Detection rules per AI tool. Each provider matches via Clue 1 (process name), Clue 2 (argv keyword), or Clue 3 (network endpoint).

argumentRuntimePrefixes.description: Process-name prefixes whose argv we scan for AI keywords. Generic node/python/Code Helper hosts go here; everything else short-circuits without an argv read for performance.

localhostEndpoints.description: Localhost (host, port) pairs that strongly imply a local LLM runtime is running, regardless of process name.

editorExtensionFolders.description: User-folder path fragments that mark a process as belonging to a parent editor (Visual Studio Code, Cursor, Kiro, etc.). Used by the parent-app resolver when no .app bundle exists in argv[0].

app.description: Optional app-update metadata. The running AgentWatch compares its CFBundleShortVersionString against latestVersion (soft prompt) and minimumVersion (hard block). Older app builds that predate this field simply ignore it.

latestVersion.description: Newest published app version (dotted decimal, e.g. '1.0.2'). Triggers an update banner when current < latest.

minimumVersion.description: Hard floor — running apps below this version are forced to update via a blocking overlay. Optional; when absent, no version is forced.

downloadURL.description: URL the in-app 'Download' button opens. Typically a GitHub Releases /latest pointer so we don't have to update the registry per release.

releaseNotes.description: Optional changelog blurb shown inside the update banner.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier — never reused, never changed. Used in app code for analytics or feature gates.

displayName.description: User-facing label shown in the AgentWatch UI.

agentType.description: Classification bucket; controls icon and grouping. Falls back to 'unknown' when a provider doesn't fit a named bucket.

processNames.description: Exact matches against kinfo_proc.kp_proc.p_comm. Note: p_comm truncates to 16 chars — entries longer than that will never hit, so use the truncated form (e.g. 'Code Helper (Plu' for 'Code Helper (Plugin)').

argvKeywords.description: Substrings that, when found anywhere in the joined argv, identify this provider. Only checked when the process name is in argumentRuntimePrefixes (see top-level field).

pathAnchors.description: Stricter alternative to argvKeywords for collision-prone names. A process matches this provider only if at least one anchor is present somewhere in argv. Use for keywords that would otherwise false-match unrelated tools (e.g. 'goose' the SQL migrator vs Block's Goose CLI).

extensionPaths.description: Path fragments characteristic of an editor extension's install location. Adds another way to identify VS Code / Cursor extensions even when the keyword matcher misses.

openFilePatterns.description: Substrings matched against the canonical paths of vnode-backed file descriptors held by the process (PROC_PIDFDVNODEPATHINFO). Catches runtimes that hide their argv via process.title rewrites — the kernel still tracks which script the runtime loaded, so e.g. Claude Code's `node` PID will have '@anthropic-ai/claude-code/cli.js' in its fd table even after argv has been mangled. Only checked when the process name is in argumentRuntimePrefixes.

envKeywords.description: Environment variable *keys* matched against the process env. Each entry is either an exact key (e.g. 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY') or a prefix glob with trailing '*' (e.g. 'CLAUDE_CODE_*' matches any key starting with that string). Values are never compared, so we don't surface secrets. Low weight in the scorer (15) — never classifies alone, only corroborates other signals so a developer with the key exported globally doesn't trip every shell descendant.

ipRanges.description: IPv4 CIDR blocks for this provider's API endpoints. Used by Clue 3 (network detection).

ipRangeConfidence.description: How sure we are about ipRanges. 'high' = observed in our own scans / officially documented; 'medium' = publicly reported; 'low' = likely-but-unverified.

confidence.description: Overall confidence that this provider's signatures are accurate and current. Tier-3 entries from research start as 'low' and get promoted as we verify.

aliases.description: Past names this provider went by. Helps when a tool rebrands (e.g. Codeium → Windsurf Plugin) — UI can fall back to the alias for legacy installs.

notes.description: Free-form note for registry maintainers — not displayed in the app. Use for caveats, deprecation status, sources.

icon.description: Icon descriptor. v1.1 uses unicode glyphs; iconURL is reserved for v1.2+ when we add real provider logos (subject to per-vendor trademark review).

glyph.description: Single unicode character or short string used as a fallback icon.

iconURL.description: Reserved. Will hold an https URL to a square PNG/SVG once v1.2 lands. Null for now.

label.description: Short human-readable name used as the matched-provider tag (e.g. 'Ollama', 'LM Studio').

pathFragment.description: Substring matched against argv[0]. Must include the leading slash and the 'extensions' segment to avoid false positives, e.g. '/.vscode/extensions/'.

parentAppName.description: Display name to use as the parent app when this fragment matches.
